summary: The test suite is well-structured with 378 passing tests across 38 test files, providing strong coverage of all major subsystems (engine, models, persistence, CLI, GUI, steps). Test organization follows a clear pattern with both unit and integration tests, and security-relevant paths are well-covered. However, there are notable coverage gaps in error/edge-case paths (catch-all exception handlers, parallel branch failures, unknown status codes), test organization issues (duplicated helpers, round-based test files), and minor fragilities (time.sleep for ordering, private attribute access).

findings.title: No dedicated tests for discovery.py module
findings.description: The discovery.py module (shared workflow discovery logic) has no dedicated test file. It is exercised indirectly through test_cli_flows.py and test_gui_discover.py, but those tests use monkeypatching and mock the discovery module differently. The shared discover_workflows() function in discovery.py is not directly tested for its own contract: search_dirs=None default behavior, handling of non-existent directories, deduplication by resolved path, or error resilience when YAML files are unreadable.
findings.recommendation: Add a test_discovery.py that directly tests discover_workflows() with controlled search_dirs, covering: empty dirs, duplicate paths, unreadable files, mixed valid/invalid YAML, and the default search_dirs=None path.

findings.title: No test for run_workflow/resume_workflow internal error catch-all handlers
findings.description: The run_workflow() function (line 233) and resume_workflow() (line 367) both have catch-all 'except Exception' handlers that wrap unexpected errors into ERR_INTERNAL envelopes with exit_code 90. These handlers are critical safety nets but are never exercised by any test. If the error wrapping logic had a bug (e.g., serialization failure), it would go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add a test that mocks an internal function (e.g., Store.__init__) to raise an unexpected exception and verify the ERR_INTERNAL envelope is returned with exit_code 90.

findings.title: Missing tests for concurrent parallel branch edge cases
findings.description: The parallel_step tests cover basic success, branch failure, and branch-not-found cases, but do not test: (1) all branches failing simultaneously, (2) exception propagation from ThreadPoolExecutor (lines 77-78 in parallel_step.py where future.result() can raise), (3) parallel execution with more than 2 branches, or (4) empty branches list.
findings.recommendation: Add tests for: all-branches-failing (verify error message aggregation), 3+ parallel branches, and a branch that raises an unexpected exception to verify the error handling in the executor loop.

findings.title: Implicit workflow completion (no end step) untested at runner level
findings.description: The _execute_steps function has a fallback path (runner.py lines 212-218) for workflows that complete without an explicit end step -- it sets status to 'completed' and returns a success envelope. While foreach and workflow_ref exercise this path indirectly, there is no direct runner-level integration test with a workflow that has only CLI steps and no end step.
findings.recommendation: Add a test: workflow with only a cli step (no end step), verify run_workflow returns ok=True with status='completed' and exit_code=0.

findings.title: No test for --version CLI flag
findings.description: The _version_callback (cli.py lines 33-36) and the --version/-v option are untested. While trivial, every other CLI command has at least a help smoke test. The version callback uses typer.Exit() with no code, which is a distinct code path from _emit().
findings.recommendation: Add: result = runner.invoke(app, ['--version']); assert result.exit_code == 0; assert __version__ in result.stdout

findings.title: Duplicate helper functions across test files
findings.description: Several test files define nearly identical helper functions: _write_await_workflow appears in test_runner_resume.py, test_cli_cancel.py, test_cli_resume.py, and test_queries.py with slight variations. _make_completed_run appears in test_delete_run.py, test_gui_server.py, and test_gui_bulk_delete.py. _run_to_waiting appears in test_runner_resume.py, test_cli_cancel.py, and test_cli_resume.py. This duplication increases maintenance burden.
findings.recommendation: Extract the most common helpers into conftest.py as shared fixtures or a tests/helpers.py module. The existing write_workflow factory fixture is a good pattern to extend.

findings.title: Test files organized by bug-fix round rather than feature area
findings.description: test_coverage_gaps.py, test_bugfixes_round2.py, test_feedback_fixes.py, and test_security_fixes.py are organized by when bugs were fixed rather than by feature area. End-step interpolation is tested in test_bugfixes_round2.py and test_feedback_fixes.py rather than test_step_end.py. This makes it harder to find all tests for a given feature and assess coverage by reading test file names.
findings.recommendation: Consider gradually migrating tests from round-based files into feature-based files. For example, move end-step interpolation tests into test_step_end.py, and shell tests into test_step_cli.py.

findings.title: time.sleep used for timestamp ordering in persistence tests
findings.description: test_list_runs_ordered_desc (test_persistence_store.py:91) uses time.sleep(0.02) to ensure distinct timestamps, and test_updated_at_changes_on_update (line 337) uses time.sleep(0.01). These are fragile on fast systems or under heavy load.
findings.recommendation: Consider mocking Store._now() to return controlled timestamps, or accept that >= comparison (already used in line 340) is sufficient without sleep.

findings.title: Platform-specific test skip hides coverage on CI
findings.description: test_foreach_body_with_nested_foreach is skipped on non-Windows with reason 'echo syntax is platform-specific'. Since the project targets Python 3.11+ and most CI runs on Linux, this test never runs in CI. The test could be rewritten with python -c to be cross-platform.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite to use a platform-agnostic command like 'python -c "print(...)"' instead of relying on echo syntax differences.

findings.title: No test for _status_to_exit_code with unknown status value
findings.description: The _status_to_exit_code function in queries.py (line 160) returns ExitCode.SUCCESS for unknown status values via the dict .get() default. This implicit behavior is not tested. If a new status like 'timeout' were added without updating this mapping, it would silently return success exit code.
findings.recommendation: Add a test asserting _status_to_exit_code('unknown') returns ExitCode.SUCCESS (documenting intentional fallback), or change the implementation to raise for unknown statuses.

findings.title: run_ctx fixture name suggests it returns a context, not a factory
findings.description: The run_ctx conftest fixture returns a Callable that creates RunContext instances. Every test that uses it must call run_ctx() with parentheses. The naming could mislead readers into thinking it returns a RunContext directly.
findings.recommendation: Consider renaming to make_run_ctx or run_ctx_factory to clarify the factory pattern.

findings.title: Tests depend on examples/ directory files existing
findings.description: Several tests (test_schema.py, test_coverage_gaps.py, test_models_workflow.py, test_cli_validate.py) reference files in examples/ like 'examples/publish-confluence-change.yaml'. These rely on files checked into the repo. If those files are renamed or removed, tests break.
findings.recommendation: This is acceptable for regression tests, but consider marking them with @pytest.mark.examples so they can be identified and optionally skipped.
